meta.title: Seedance 2 API | Multimodal Video Generation, Native Audio-Video, References & 1080p | APIDot
meta.description: Use Seedance 2 on APIDot for multimodal video generation with native audio-video output, image or video references, multi-shot continuity, and async production delivery. Choose between `seedance-2` and `seedance-2-fast` based on quality and speed.

hero.title: Seedance 2 API for Multimodal Video Generation
hero.description: Teams choose Seedance 2 when they need more than prompt-only video generation, especially for reference-driven scenes, native audio-video output, and short-form clips that need continuity across shots. On APIDot, both `seedance-2` and `seedance-2-fast` use the same async workflow, so you can iterate in Fast mode and move the same setup into standard delivery when quality matters more.

overview.title: Choose Seedance 2 when the video needs references, audio, and tighter control over the final clip
paragraphs: Seedance 2 is a stronger fit when a plain text-to-video model is not enough, especially for product ads, storyboard tests, localized variants, and short-form branded video where references, camera intent, and audio matter to the final result. It is built for teams that want a more directed generation workflow instead of hoping one prompt will carry everything.

On this page, the real workflow boundary is clear: `seedance-2` is the higher-end path and the only variant here that supports 1080p, while `seedance-2-fast` is for lower-cost iteration. You can control the clip with first and last frame images or with `reference_*_urls`, but those two approaches cannot be mixed in the same request.

APIDot keeps the model practical for production use. Submit once through the shared async endpoint, poll or use webhook callbacks, and move the same validated playground settings into your application without redesigning the workflow around a different API.

capabilities.title: What Seedance 2 API is worth using for

items.title: Native audio-video generation
items.description: This matters when the clip needs to land as a finished asset instead of a silent draft that depends on extra post work. Seedance 2 on APIDot can generate synchronized video and audio together, which makes it more useful for dialogue, rhythm, ambience, and lip-sync driven work.

items.title: First/last frame and multimodal reference control
items.description: Teams look for this when they want the output to stay closer to a visual brief rather than drift from a text prompt alone. APIDot supports first and last frame control through `image_urls`, or multimodal reference mode through image, video, and audio reference fields.

items.title: Multi-shot storytelling and continuity
items.description: This is one of the more valuable reasons to use Seedance 2 for short-form narrative or branded clips. It is better suited to sequences that need character continuity, stable style, and cleaner transitions across multiple shots in one generated result.

items.title: Camera motion, pacing, and cinematic control
items.description: People search for Seedance 2 when they need stronger control over movement, rhythm, and the overall feel of the scene. On APIDot, it is a good fit for prompts that specify shot behavior, pacing, and visual intent instead of only describing the subject.

items.title: Standard vs Fast variants with 1080p delivery path
items.description: The variant split matters because not every video job needs the same cost or finish level. Use `seedance-2-fast` for quicker iteration at 480p or 720p, and move to `seedance-2` when you need the standard model path and 1080p output.

items.title: Unified async workflow for production use
items.description: A useful video model still needs a clean delivery path into real systems. APIDot keeps both Seedance 2 variants on the same async submit, polling, and webhook lifecycle, so test settings can move into production with less friction.

useCases.title: What teams actually use Seedance 2 API for
useCases.description: This page is best for teams that need reference-driven video generation, native audio, and an async workflow they can carry from testing into production.
items: Product ads and social-first branded videos
Storyboard tests, shot planning, and previs
Localized marketing variants with native lip-sync
Training and explainer videos with consistent presenters
Music videos, dance clips, and cinematic b-roll

quickstart.title: Start using {modelName} on APIDot in three steps

steps.title: Create your API key
steps.description: Generate an API key in your dashboard and get immediate access to Seedance 2 through APIDot without waitlists or manual approval.

steps.title: Top up credits
steps.description: Billing is usage-based per second. Add credits based on the model, resolution, and whether your request includes video input.

steps.title: Integrate the async workflow
steps.description: Submit model="{activeModelId}" requests to {endpoint}, then poll status or consume webhook callbacks to retrieve the final media output in your app.

items.question: What is Seedance 2 on APIDot?
items.answer: Seedance 2 on APIDot is a multimodal video generation workflow built around `seedance-2` and `seedance-2-fast`. It is meant for short-form video work where prompts alone are not enough and the final clip benefits from visual references, native audio, continuity, and tighter shot control.

items.question: What inputs and reference modes does Seedance 2 support?
items.answer: You start with a text prompt and can optionally add first and last frame images through `image_urls`, or switch to multimodal reference mode with `reference_image_urls`, `reference_video_urls`, and `reference_audio_urls`. Those two reference approaches cannot be used together in the same request.

items.question: What are the duration, resolution, and aspect-ratio limits?
items.answer: This page supports integer durations from 4 to 15 seconds. `seedance-2` supports `480p`, `720p`, and `1080p`, while `seedance-2-fast` supports `480p` and `720p`. Supported aspect ratios on APIDot are `21:9`, `16:9`, `4:3`, `1:1`, `3:4`, and `9:16`.

items.question: Does Seedance 2 support native audio and lip-sync?
items.answer: Yes. Native audio-video generation is one of the main reasons to use Seedance 2. It is designed for clips where dialogue, ambience, rhythm, or lip-sync should be generated together with the video instead of being bolted on later.

items.question: How do first/last frame mode and reference mode differ?
items.answer: First and last frame mode is for guiding the opening and ending visual state of the clip with up to two images. Reference mode is for broader multimodal control through image, video, or audio reference inputs. On APIDot, you choose one approach or the other per request.

items.question: How should I choose `seedance-2` vs `seedance-2-fast`?
items.answer: Use `seedance-2-fast` when you want cheaper, faster iteration at 480p or 720p. Use `seedance-2` when the clip is closer to delivery quality, when you need the standard model path, or when you need 1080p output.

items.question: How is Seedance 2 billed on APIDot?
items.answer: Seedance 2 is billed per second on APIDot. The cost depends on the model variant, the selected resolution, and whether your request includes video input, so pricing is not a single flat rate across all Seedance 2 jobs.

items.question: Is Seedance 2 suitable for commercial or production use?
items.answer: Yes. APIDot exposes Seedance 2 through a production-ready async API with submit, polling, and webhook support. That makes it usable in creative tools, content pipelines, and automation systems, while commercial rights still depend on your platform terms.
